what_is_it: A group of ingredients that help fat and water mix together.
why_used: They improve texture and keep products smooth and stable.
health_impact: Usually considered acceptable in small amounts, but they are still part of processed foods.
fun_fact: They are often used in chocolate and spreads.

what_is_it: An emulsifier made from oats.
why_used: It helps ingredients mix better and keeps texture smooth.
health_impact: Generally low concern, but still a processed ingredient.
fun_fact: It is a plant-based alternative to soy lecithin.

what_is_it: An acid naturally found in citrus fruits and also made for food use.
why_used: It adds sourness and helps preserve freshness.
health_impact: Generally considered safe for most people.
fun_fact: It is one of the most common additives in food and drinks.

what_is_it: A thickener made from red seaweed.
why_used: It keeps food smooth, creamy, and stable.
health_impact: Some people prefer to limit it because it is debated in digestion-related discussions.
fun_fact: Seaweed has been used in cooking for centuries.

what_is_it: A thickener made from guar beans.
why_used: It improves texture and helps products stay evenly mixed.
health_impact: Usually tolerated well, but some people prefer fewer gums in highly processed foods.
fun_fact: It is common in sauces, desserts, and plant-based products.

what_is_it: A thickener made through fermentation.
why_used: It makes foods thicker and helps ingredients stay mixed.
health_impact: Generally considered safe, though some people prefer to limit gums in processed foods.
fun_fact: It is widely used in gluten-free foods.

what_is_it: A plant-based thickener usually made from fruit.
why_used: It helps jams, desserts, and fillings set properly.
health_impact: Generally considered safe.
fun_fact: Pectin is naturally present in apples and citrus peels.

ingredient_name_en: Mono- and diglycerides of fatty acids
aliases: E471|mono and diglycerides of fatty acids|mono-und diglycerides
what_is_it: Processed fats that help ingredients mix and stay smooth.
why_used: They improve texture and help products stay soft or creamy.
health_impact: Usually considered acceptable, but they are highly processed ingredients.
fun_fact: They are common in baked goods and desserts.

ingredient_name_en: Citric acid esters of mono- and diglycerides of fatty acids
ingredient_name_de: Citronensäureester von Mono- und Diglyceriden von Speisefettsäuren
ingredient_name_pl: estry kwasu cytrynowego mono- i diglicerydów kwasów tłuszczowych
aliases: E472c|citric acid esters of mono- and diglycerides of fatty acids
what_is_it: An emulsifier made from fats and citric acid.
why_used: It keeps texture smooth and helps ingredients stay mixed.
health_impact: Usually considered acceptable, but it is a processed additive.
fun_fact: It is often used in bakery and dessert products.

what_is_it: An emulsifier often used in chocolate.
why_used: It makes chocolate smoother and easier to process.
health_impact: Usually considered acceptable in small amounts, but it is highly processed.
fun_fact: It helps make chocolate flow better during manufacturing.

what_is_it: A group of mineral salts used in food.
why_used: They help dough rise and can control acidity.
health_impact: Generally considered safe.
fun_fact: This group includes baking soda-related ingredients.

what_is_it: A flavour enhancer that boosts savory taste.
why_used: It makes food taste stronger and more umami-rich.
health_impact: Approved for use, but some people prefer to limit it.
fun_fact: MSG is famous for its strong umami effect.

what_is_it: A high-intensity artificial sweetener.
why_used: It makes foods and drinks sweet without adding sugar.
health_impact: Often used in low-sugar products; some people prefer to limit artificial sweeteners.
fun_fact: It is much sweeter than regular sugar.

what_is_it: A low-calorie artificial sweetener.
why_used: It replaces sugar in diet products.
health_impact: Approved for use, but it is one of the most debated sweeteners.
fun_fact: It is commonly found in sugar-free drinks and gum.

what_is_it: A group of artificial sweeteners.
why_used: They add sweetness without much energy.
health_impact: Some people prefer to avoid artificial sweeteners even when approved.
fun_fact: They are often used in tabletop sweeteners.

what_is_it: A sugar alcohol used as a sweetener.
why_used: It sweetens food with fewer calories than sugar.
health_impact: Large amounts may cause digestive discomfort in some people.
fun_fact: It is common in sugar-free candies.

what_is_it: A very strong artificial sweetener.
why_used: It adds sweetness without much energy.
health_impact: Approved for use, but often avoided by people who prefer fewer artificial sweeteners.
fun_fact: It is much sweeter than sugar.

what_is_it: A high-intensity sweetener made from sugar chemistry.
why_used: It sweetens foods without adding much energy.
health_impact: Often used in low-sugar products; some people prefer to limit artificial sweeteners.
fun_fact: It stays sweet even when heated.

what_is_it: A sweetener from the stevia plant.
why_used: It adds sweetness without regular sugar.
health_impact: Often seen as a more natural sweetener, though taste and tolerance vary by person.
fun_fact: It can taste much sweeter than sugar.

what_is_it: A group of sugar alcohol sweeteners.
why_used: They replace sugar in reduced-sugar foods.
health_impact: Large amounts may cause bloating or digestive discomfort in some people.
fun_fact: They are common in sugar-free chocolate.

what_is_it: A sugar alcohol used as a sweetener.
why_used: It adds sweetness with fewer calories than sugar.
health_impact: Too much may upset digestion; it is also dangerous for dogs.
fun_fact: It is common in chewing gum and mints.

what_is_it: A sugar alcohol sweetener with very low calories.
why_used: It adds sweetness without regular sugar.
health_impact: Often tolerated better than some other polyols, but still may cause discomfort in large amounts.
fun_fact: It is popular in keto-style products.

what_is_it: A form of vitamin C used in food.
why_used: It helps prevent oxidation and keeps products fresh.
health_impact: Generally considered safe.
fun_fact: It can also help bread dough behave better.

what_is_it: A sodium form of vitamin C.
why_used: It helps keep foods fresh and stable.
health_impact: Generally considered safe.
fun_fact: It is often used in processed meats and drinks.

what_is_it: A natural antioxidant mixture related to vitamin E.
why_used: It helps protect fats from going rancid.
health_impact: Generally considered safe.
fun_fact: It is often used to protect oils and snacks.

what_is_it: A synthetic antioxidant.
why_used: It helps fats stay fresh for longer.
health_impact: Many people prefer to limit synthetic preservatives and antioxidants.
fun_fact: It is used to slow down spoilage in fatty foods.

what_is_it: A synthetic antioxidant.
why_used: It helps prevent fats from spoiling.
health_impact: Many people prefer to limit synthetic preservatives and antioxidants.
fun_fact: It is often discussed together with BHA.

what_is_it: A preservative used to stop mold and yeast growth.
why_used: It helps foods last longer.
health_impact: Generally approved, but some users prefer fewer preservatives.
fun_fact: It is common in baked goods and dairy products.

what_is_it: A preservative that helps stop mold and yeast.
why_used: It extends shelf life in many packaged foods.
health_impact: Generally approved, but often avoided by people seeking fewer additives.
fun_fact: It is one of the most common preservatives in food.

what_is_it: A preservative used to control spoilage.
why_used: It helps products last longer.
health_impact: Approved for use, though some people prefer to limit preservatives.
fun_fact: It is often used in acidic foods and drinks.

what_is_it: A preservative made from benzoic acid.
why_used: It helps prevent spoilage in drinks and sauces.
health_impact: Approved for use, though some users prefer fewer preservatives.
fun_fact: It is common in soft drinks and condiments.

what_is_it: A preservative gas used in some foods and drinks.
why_used: It helps stop spoilage and discoloration.
health_impact: Some people are sensitive to sulphites, so this ingredient gets extra attention.
fun_fact: It is often associated with dried fruit and wine.

what_is_it: A curing salt used in processed meats.
why_used: It helps preserve meat and maintain color.
health_impact: Often recommended to limit because it is associated with highly processed meat products.
fun_fact: It helps keep cured meats pink.

what_is_it: A preservative used in some cured foods.
why_used: It helps preserve products and support curing.
health_impact: Often recommended to limit because it is associated with processed meat products.
fun_fact: It can be converted during food processing or digestion.

what_is_it: The main acid found in vinegar.
why_used: It adds acidity and helps preserve food.
health_impact: Generally considered safe.
fun_fact: It gives vinegar its sharp taste.

what_is_it: An acid produced naturally by fermentation.
why_used: It helps control acidity and preserve foods.
health_impact: Generally considered safe.
fun_fact: It is used in many fermented and packaged foods.

what_is_it: A gas used in fizzy drinks and food packaging.
why_used: It creates bubbles or helps protect food.
health_impact: Generally considered safe.
fun_fact: It is what makes sparkling water sparkle.

what_is_it: An acid used mainly in soft drinks and processed foods.
why_used: It adds sharpness and controls acidity.
health_impact: Often limited in highly processed drinks and foods.
fun_fact: It is especially common in cola-style drinks.

aliases: E392|extracts of rosemary|rosemary extract|ekstrakt z rozmarynu
what_is_it: A plant extract used as an antioxidant.
why_used: It helps keep fats and oils fresh.
health_impact: Generally considered safe.
fun_fact: It comes from the rosemary plant, but not used for flavor here.

what_is_it: A group of sugar alcohol sweeteners.
why_used: They add sweetness and moisture.
health_impact: Large amounts may cause digestive discomfort.
fun_fact: They are common in sugar-free products.

what_is_it: A sugar alcohol used as a sweetener or bulking agent.
why_used: It helps add sweetness and structure.
health_impact: Large amounts may cause digestive discomfort.
fun_fact: It is used in sugar-free sweets and coatings.

what_is_it: A syrupy ingredient that helps keep foods moist.
why_used: It prevents products from drying out too quickly.
health_impact: Generally considered safe.
fun_fact: It can also make texture feel smoother.

what_is_it: An emulsifier that helps ingredients stay mixed.
why_used: It keeps texture smooth and stable.
health_impact: Usually approved, but some people prefer fewer highly processed emulsifiers.
fun_fact: It is used in many desserts and sauces.

what_is_it: An emulsifier used to keep foods smooth and evenly mixed.
why_used: It improves texture and stability.
health_impact: Usually approved, but often seen as a more processed additive.
fun_fact: It belongs to the polysorbate family.

what_is_it: An emulsifier that keeps ingredients blended together.
why_used: It improves texture in creamy foods.
health_impact: Usually approved, but still a processed additive.
fun_fact: It is another member of the polysorbate family.

what_is_it: An emulsifier used in some processed foods.
why_used: It helps ingredients stay mixed and texture stay stable.
health_impact: Usually approved, but often grouped with other processed emulsifiers.
fun_fact: It is less commonly seen than polysorbate 80.
